title: Coffee on Wool
timeEstimate: Pro-lane within hours; one home round max
stainChemistry: Coffee = browning pigments + tannins + (if cream) milk milk protein + butterfat. Wool is wool protein with fiber bonds. The two failures stack on wool exactly like with red wine: heat felts the fiber permanently AND sets tannin, alkaline cleaners weaken keratin AND lock tannin, agitation when wet felts on contact. Coffee with cream is harder than black on wool because the milk protein adds protein-before-heat ordering — and on wool you can't safely deploy enough chemistry to address both. Same one-round home ceiling as red-wine-wool.
whyThisWorks: A wool specialist with cold NSD + timed acetic acid + dilute peroxide can lift fresh coffee tannin without felting. Home ceiling is one careful cold-water round. Repeat home rounds on wool felt the fiber more often than they lift more coffee.
defaultAssumption: Wool + coffee = pro within hours. ONE home round max, then stop. Coffee with cream = even earlier escalation (protein adds complexity).

spottingProtocol.instruction: Press cold water on the BACK with vertical pressure only. No rubbing — agitates wool, felts fiber. Replace cloth as it picks up brown.
Cold NSD sponge press only. Suspends remaining tannin without alkalinity that weakens wool.
Rinse until water runs clear. Residual NSD interferes with later steps.
For residual color, diluted acetic acid via dropper. STRICT 60-sec dwell. Flush cold immediately — wool weakens with prolonged acid.

spottingProtocol.agent: Dilute Hydrogen Peroxide for Oxidized Residue
spottingProtocol.instruction: For oxidized brown, dilute peroxide 1–2 min monitored. Flush cold immediately.
Rinse until clear. Press between dry towels — never wring. Lay flat to air-dry, reshape damp.

steps: 5. Dilute H₂O₂ for residual brown, 1–2 min monitored.
6. Final cold rinse, press flat to air-dry.
professionalProtocol.temperature: cold throughout — wool felts above 30°C when wet
warnings: NEVER hot water — felts wool + sets tannin
60-sec ceiling on acid; 1–2 min on peroxide

homeSolutions.agent: ONE Round Max — Felt Risk Is the Real Failure
homeSolutions.instruction: Wool's worst home failure isn't "the stain didn't come out" — it's felting from repeat chemistry. Wool felts permanently when wet wool is agitated, when temp goes over 30°C, when alkaline cleaners open the scales. The one-round rule is non-negotiable. If your one careful cold-water round doesn't clear it, pro is the answer. Don't repeat.

homeSolutions.agent: Cold Water Press-Blot from the BACK
homeSolutions.instruction: Turn inside out. Place white towel under the stain. Press a SECOND white cloth dampened in COLD water onto the BACK. Vertical pressure only — no rubbing, no scrubbing. Lift, refresh cloth, press again. Cold ONLY — heat felts wool AND sets coffee tannin. Both failures stack at warm + agitate.

If brown remains after cold water alone: half teaspoon Eucalan or Soak (wool-specific, enzyme-free, pH-neutral) in two cups COLD water. Dip a cloth, wring nearly dry, PRESS on the stain. 2–3 minutes dwell. ONE PASS. Do NOT use OxiClean, Biz, or regular detergent — alkaline opens scales and locks tannin.

Press wool between two dry white towels — stack, press, lift. No wringing or twisting. Lay flat on fresh towel, reshape damp, air-dry at room temperature out of sun. NEVER hang wet wool. NEVER apply heat — even residual heat felts the fiber.

homeSolutions.agent: Pro Within Hours If Anything Remains
homeSolutions.instruction: If brown remains after one careful round, STOP. Take to a wool specialist (call ahead). Tell them: "Coffee on wool, [black / with cream], age [hours], one round at home with cold water + [Eucalan]. No hot water, no enzymes, no OxiClean."

steps: 1. Wool + coffee = ONE home round max.
2. Cold water press-blot from the BACK (no rubbing).
3. Optional one pass with Eucalan / Soak, 2–3 min cold.
4. Press dry between towels, lay flat air-dry.
5. Anything remaining = pro. NO second home round.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop after one careful round — repeat home chemistry on wool felts the fiber.
warnings: NEVER hot water — felts wool + sets tannin
NEVER wring or hang wet wool

neverDo: Never agitate or scrub wet wool
fiberSensitivities: Wool keratin felts when wet + warm + agitated
fiber-bond lock together irreversibly under heat

materialWarnings: ONE round home max — repeat chemistry felts wool
Coffee with cream = earlier pro escalation (protein layer)

escalation.when: After one careful home round, regardless of result. Earlier for coffee with cream, large spills, or high-value garments.
escalation.whatToTell: "Coffee on wool, [black / with cream], age [hours], one round at home with cold water + [wool-safe detergent]. No hot water, no enzymes. [Residual brown / fully clear]."
escalation.specialistType: professional dry cleaner specializing in wool (call ahead)

contextQuestions: Black coffee or coffee with cream?
How long ago — fresh, hours, or older?
Wool type — merino / regular / felt / blend?
Has anyone used hot water / OxiClean / agitation already?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Coffee with cream adds milk protein — different ordering (cold flush, then handle protein BEFORE tannin chemistry). Merino is finer + felts faster. Pre-applied OxiClean or hot-water = wool may already be partially compromised; disclose to pro.
